boundaries.title_guess: Cornwall: St Ives, Lands End, the Lizard

evidence.excerpt: May 3rd Tuesday ... We drove on to St. Ives & camped at Ayr ... pretty beach & gorgeous turquoise sea
We went to lands End ... We carried on to Mullions Cove ... We left to go down the Lizard further

boundaries.notes: Trip already in progress at the start of the window (continues from previous window). Matches book2 index p.35-40 St Columb/Truro/St Ives/St Just/Geevor/Lands End/Lizard/Trebah/Saltash (1994).

boundaries.title_guess: Brecon Beacons family weekend (Blaensan, Ystradfellte, Cwm Llwch, Tretower)

evidence.excerpt: Saturday May 7 ... On to home up the I75.
Friday May 27th ... Simon, Eve, William, Peter, John, Charles ... set off at 3.45. for Poltern ... we try a new Welsh campsite Blaensan

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0126_L ends the Cornwall trip ('On to home up the I75.') then immediately starts a new Welsh family weekend at the 'Friday May 27th' heading.
boundaries.notes: Welsh Brecon Beacons weekend with extended family; Mountain Centre, Porth-yr-Ogof, Cwm Llwch, Tretower. Continues A_0127-A_0129_L (A_0128 duplicates A_0127). Matches book2 index p.41-42 Ystradfellte / Cwm Llwch Bwlch, Tretower (1994).

evidence.excerpt: June 25th Sat 3.0. We left Birmingham to come to the Wolverhampton camping shop ... drove onto a lovely open park land 6 miles from Stourbridge.

boundaries.notes: Short weekend outing leaving Birmingham. The previous Tretower/Cwm Llwch trip closes on A_0129_L ('Tuesday'); this departs fresh on the same right-hand page. Likely part of book2 index p.43 'Nr. Stourbridge, Malvern' (1994). Could be read as one rolling sequence with the following Malvern weekend; reported separately because of the distinct home-departure heading.

evidence.excerpt: July 1st Friday Simon, Eve, William, Peter, John & Charles set off to Malvern for the weekend ... We camped at Shuttlefast Farm

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0129_R carries two distinct departures: 'June 25th Sat' (Stourbridge) and 'July 1st Friday' (Malvern). The page contains the end of the Stourbridge weekend and the start of the Malvern weekend.
boundaries.notes: Malvern weekend; Wimbledon-watching, lunch with Murtagh, camped Shuttlefast Farm (detour via Redditch). Continues to A_0130_L 'Sunday ... then drove home'. Book2 index p.43 Malvern (1994).

evidence.excerpt: Sunday We packed up + went to Malvern chapel ... then drove home
Mon 18th July ... We picked up Samra Kolon in the City Centre above the Mosque at 7.25!. + drove on to Thorpenes.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0130_L ends the Malvern weekend ('then drove home') and starts the Thorpeness trip at the 'Mon 18th July' heading.
boundaries.notes: East Anglia trip via the new A14: breakfast nr Naseby, then a week at Thorpeness with friends. Continues to A_0130_R 'We went home on Sunday morning.' Book2 index p.43 Thorpeness (1994).

boundaries.title_guess: France: Picardy, Champagne & Burgundy (Laon, Troyes, Vezelay, Autun, St Jean de Vaux)

evidence.excerpt: We went home on Sunday morning.
Sat July 30th 6 of us set off at 8.40 ... motored down to The Nook *** Women's World ... Twenty minutes from Dover. We left at 8.40. + caught the ferry
[hand-drawn map of France: Calais, St. Omer, Laon, Paris, Troyes, Bar sur Aube, Clairvaux, Vezelay, Morvan, Autun, Chalon-s-Saone] 1994

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0130_R ends the Thorpeness trip ('We went home on Sunday morning.') then starts the France trip at 'Sat July 30th' (Snape concert, The Nook campsite nr Dover, ferry).
boundaries.notes: Long France trip Aug 1994: Laon, Vauclair, Pontavert, Troyes, Vezelay, Autun, St Jean de Vaux, Givry, Chalon-sur-Saone, Clairvaux, Ste-Marie-du-lac, Lumbres. Matches book2 index p.44-51 (1994). Returns home A_0138_R 'Home again!' (Saturday 13th Aug).

evidence.excerpt: Home again! John's birthday
5.10. We set off to Beauchamp to hear Thomas. Shostakovich Concerto ... On the Saturday William did a maths paper + ... walked up to look at the trains

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0138_R ends the France trip ('Home again!') and begins a short Beauchamp weekend outing ('We set off to Beauchamp to hear Thomas').
boundaries.notes: Short weekend stays at Beauchamp (Gloucester) for the boys' concerts; continues into A_0139_L (Grandma to lunch, Thomas plays Haydn). Book2 index p.52 'Beauchamp x 2' (1994). Two consecutive Beauchamp weekends are run together in the narrative; treated as one boundary because they are back-to-back without an explicit home return between them.

boundaries.title_guess: North Wales: Roman Steps, Rhinog, Harlech

evidence.excerpt: Sat Sept 3rd We left at 8.30. + breakfasted at the Wolverhampton camping shop ... 1.55. We reached [?Limes] + had lunch.
Sun Sept 4. ... walk up the Roman Steps ... Simon, Thomas, Peter + John set off for Rhinog ... We went to Harlech.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0139_L holds the tail of the Beauchamp concert weekend (Aug 26 / Sat 27 entries) then opens the North Wales trip at 'Sat Sept 3rd We left at 8.30.'
boundaries.notes: Wales weekend: Roman Steps, Rhinog, Harlech (Welsh Evensong), Machynlleth on the way home. Continues A_0139_R-A_0140_L. Matches book2 index p.53 'Dinas, Roman Steps, Harlech' (1994).

evidence.excerpt: Tues 6th Sept. ... On the way home we stopped at Machynlleth Centre for Alternative Technology ... we went [home?]
Saturday September 24th ... we have come out for the day to Lichfield & Cannock Chase. We breakfasted in the van in the Cathedral Precinct.

boundaries.notes: Single-day outing to Lichfield Cathedral and Cannock Chase ('come out for the day'). Boundary falls cleanly at the A_0140_R 'Saturday September 24th' heading; the Wales trip closes at the foot of A_0140_L. Matches book2 index p.54 'Lichfield + Cannock Chase, Lee Valley' (1994).

evidence.excerpt: Wednesday, 26th October ... we decide to leave home anyway. At 4.35 we set off the 7 of us, for Chingford Lee Valley ... going to London to choose sofas + visit the Tower of London ... before driving on to Cambridge
Fri 28th Oct + Sat ... We decide to drive on to Cambridge. We see St. John's, Kettle's Yard ... go to Evensong at King's ... come home a day early

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0141_L holds the close of the Cannock Chase day out ('a viper. William threw stones at trees ...') then opens the Lee Valley/London/Cambridge trip at the 'Wednesday, 26th October' heading.
boundaries.notes: Half-term trip: Lee Valley campsite at Chingford, the Tower of London (Crown Jewels), then Cambridge (King's Evensong, Kettle's Yard); illness-plagued. Returns home a day early (A_0142_L). Matches book2 index p.54 Lee Valley (1994).

evidence.excerpt: April 21st 1995. Friday 5.45pm we left for the west. We stayed at Stoke Works. *** ... near Droitwich

boundaries.notes: Start of the 1995 season; year clearly stated 'April 21st 1995'. Short weekend at Stoke Works near Droitwich (canal, leisure centre), with a Sunday trip to Malvern to drop Bertrand at the airport (A_0143_R). Matches book3 index p.55-area 'Stoke Works' (1995 marker). Note: book2 index also lists p.55 'Cambridge Stoke Works' 1995.

boundaries.title_guess: Lake District (Rydal, Coniston/Brantwood, Eskdale, Wast Water, Grasmere)

evidence.excerpt: April 27th Friday At 6.40 pm Simon & Eve left for a holiday in the Lake District ... We drove north ... Foxcovert Farm campsite *** at 8.15.
We left after reading after lunch + drove a short distance to Coniston ... camped at Pier Cottage ... Brantwood, on the eastern shore.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0143_R holds the close of the Stoke Works weekend (Apr 23 Malvern/airport drop) then opens the Lake District holiday at the 'April 27th Friday' heading ('Simon & Eve left for a holiday in the Lake District').
boundaries.notes: Parents-only Lake District holiday (children at school/boarding): Foxcovert Farm nr Knutsford, Rydal Water, Hawkshead, Coniston (Gondola, Brantwood), Eskdale/Boot, Hardknott fort, Wast Water, Grasmere/Dove Cottage, Troutbeck/Townend; meals at Rothay Manor and White Moss House. Matches book2 index p.57-62 (1995). Heads for home A_0150_L 'At 4.30 we set off for home.'

evidence.excerpt: At 4.30 we set off for home.
Saturday May 27th We left, the 7 of us at 8.20 ... only arrived at Upper Cantref Farm at 12.00. The boys played cricket.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0150_R holds the tail of the Lake District trip (Dove Cottage/Wordsworth notes, supper at Jonathan's) then opens a new Welsh weekend at 'Saturday May 27th We left, the 7 of us'.
boundaries.notes: New trip to Upper Cantref Farm (Llanfrynach / Brecon area); opens at the end of the window and runs past it. Matches book2 index p.63 'Upper Cantref' (1995).

open_at_end: trip starting A_0150_R (Upper Cantref Farm, Brecon, 1995) opens at the foot of the window and continues into the next window
notes: Transcribed spreads cover A_0121-A_0150 (the book metadata range A_0084-A_0167 is broader than the supplied transcripts). This stretch is a dense run of short 1994 weekend outings (Welsh weekends, Stourbridge, Malvern, Thorpeness, Beauchamp concert weekends, Lichfield day, Lee Valley/London/Cambridge) bracketed by two longer trips: the Cornwall trip (in progress at window start) and the Aug 1994 France trip, then the 1995 season opening with Stoke Works and a parents-only Lake District holiday. Several page transitions are mixed (end of one short trip + start of the next on the same right-hand page). A_0128 duplicates A_0127 (re-transcription of the same Brecon weekend pages); both belong to the same Brecon family weekend. Confidences are lower for the short Stourbridge/Malvern outings because the home-return between them is not always explicitly stated.
